prompt: Create a cinematic travel poster based on a realistic street photograph of two vintage Lisbon trams passing close together on sunlit tracks. The scene should feel like a warm late-afternoon European city street, with deep shadows, golden highlights, parked cars and greenery in the background, and passengers visible through the tram windows. The trams are classic red, yellow, cream, and dark trim, with wooden window frames and authentic advertising panels; include small readable details such as a Coca-Cola style script ad on the left tram and a small route number "56" on the right tram. Compose the image horizontally in a 3:2 aspect ratio, tightly cropped so the tram fronts dominate the frame and overlap near the center. Add large elegant white handwritten brush-script title text across the middle reading Lisbon, slightly slanted and spanning both trams. Beneath it, add a centered uppercase serif subtitle reading WHERE EVERY CORNER TELLS A STORY, with thin decorative horizontal lines on both sides and a small diamond ornament below. Use high-contrast travel editorial color grading, realistic photography, crisp details, natural reflections in glass, and no extra logos or watermarks beyond the tram advertisements.
